title: AgentExecutionRecordItem
description:
One bounded, independently consumable append item for an Agent Run.

This is the public storage/stream seam. It intentionally does not expose an
unbounded whole-run snapshot.

AcceptedSourceEnvelope.description:
Effective trust assigned to an unchanged source item after acceptance.

Organization, run, ingest position, and ingest time remain exclusively on
the enclosing `AgentExecutionRecordItem`.

ActuationAction.description: Allowlisted actions an enforcement point can report.

oneOf.description: Permit the subject operation to continue.
Isolate the subject from a boundary.
Apply a narrower capability or resource limit.
Emit a notification without changing execution.

ActuationReportBody.description: A report of actuation tied to the policy decision that requested it.

AgentExecutionRecordFact.description: The typed fact retained in one append-oriented record item.

oneOf.description: Immutable run opening identity and boundary.
Server-accepted source registration and effective trust.
Evidence-backed relation between the run and an execution identity.
Source evidence after acceptance and effective-trust assignment.

AgentLifecycleEvent.description: Agent lifecycle event names exposed by the structure-only contract.

AgentProtocol.description: Agent protocol families supported in v0.1.

AuthorityKind.description: The kind of boundary that authorized a run.

AuthorityRef.description: A content-free reference to the Authority that permits a run.

ClockBasis.description: Time basis supplied by an Evidence Source.

oneOf.description: A monotonic clock converted to wall time by the source.
A vendor or external provider clock.

CorrelationRefs.description: Explicit identifiers supplied for correlation; absence is never invented.

agent_ref.description: Agent or delegate identity, when supplied.

provider_ref.description: Provider-native session, job, or audit identity, when supplied.

runtime_ref.description: Process, container, Pod, VM, runner, or provider workload identity.

span_ref.description: Distributed trace span identity, when supplied.

task_ref.description: MCP or A2A task identity, when supplied.

tool_ref.description: Tool-call, MCP call, or A2A task identity, when supplied.

CoverageReasonCode.description: Stable reasons attached to a coverage computation.

oneOf.description: All requirements for the dimension were met.
A required source or lifecycle item is absent.
The source samples rather than observing every qualifying item.
The environment does not expose the required capability.
The relevant environment boundary is opaque.
An independent check disagrees with a claim.
This dimension has no requirement for the run profile.
Available evidence does not support a stronger state.

CoverageSummary.description: The three independent coverage dimensions and outcome comparison.

DelegationLifecycleBody.description: A content-off delegation relationship and lifecycle observation.

oneOf.description: A gap or degraded source state.
A source failure that prevents expected evidence.

EnvironmentKind.description: The supported environment profiles for an Agent Run.

oneOf.description: CI runner or remote development workspace.

EvidenceBoundary.description: Boundary a source can directly observe, independent from effective trust.

oneOf.description: Agent hook, wrapper, or IDE harness boundary.
In-process SDK or telemetry processor boundary.
MCP, A2A, or comparable protocol boundary.
Customer-controlled host, VM, container, or node boundary.
Independent read-back boundary for an external outcome.

EvidenceFlags.description: Loss, redaction, and content indicators carried by a source contribution.

contains_content.description: The body contains authorized content rather than structure only.

loss_detected.description: The source reports missing or dropped evidence relevant to this item.

redacted.description: At least one source field was redacted before transmission.

EvidenceObjectRef.description: Immutable reference to separately authorized evidence content.

EvidenceRef.description: An opaque reference carried instead of prompts, responses, arguments, or bodies.

oneOf.description: The controlled host boundary met its declared capability without a gap.
Execution evidence exists with a known capability or loss gap.
No qualifying source can observe the execution boundary.
The run profile requires no execution assertion.
A required execution source did not produce usable complete evidence.
The interaction was requested but not observed running.
The interaction reached a terminal observation.

ObservedTime.description: Source-observed time and its known uncertainty.

OperationOutcome.description: Bounded outcome vocabulary shared by attempted operations.

oneOf.description: The operation reached its requested terminal state.
The operation ran but did not reach its requested terminal state.
Policy or an enforcement point prevented the operation.
The operation has not reached a terminal state.
Available evidence does not establish the operation result.

OrderingCapability.description: Ordering guarantee declared by one source stream.

oneOf.description: Sequence is strictly increasing within a source stream.
The source may deliver reordered items and reports observed gaps.

OutcomeClaimBody.description: An agent or provider outcome claim without prose or result content.

OutcomeComparisonState.description: Comparison between a claim and an independent outcome check.

oneOf.description: The independent check matches the claimed outcome.
The independent check disagrees with the claimed outcome.
A definitive comparison is not currently available.
A required outcome was independently checked, whether matching or not.
An outcome was claimed but not independently checked.
A reliable claim/check relationship cannot be established.
The run profile requires no external outcome.

OutcomeVerificationBody.description: An independent comparison of an outcome claim with an opaque observation.

PolicyDecisionBody.description: A policy decision kept separate from any enforcement report.

oneOf.description: A separate approval is required before actuation.
The policy does not apply to the subject.
The source cannot establish a decision.
The policy default determined the decision.
A human approval determined the decision.
The subject request was structurally invalid.

PrincipalKind.description: The kind of authenticated identity acting within an Authority.

PrincipalRef.description: A content-free reference to the Principal acting in a run.

PrivacyCapability.description: Privacy-preserving payload representations a source can produce.

oneOf.description: Typed allowlisted metadata with no raw prompt, result, body, or argv.
Immutable object reference subject to separate capture and read policy.

ProtocolInteractionBody.description: An MCP or A2A interaction represented only by identities and metadata.

ProtocolOperation.description: Allowlisted protocol operations; they carry no request or response body.

oneOf.description: Resolve an MCP prompt template by reference.
Observe or publish an A2A task update.
Exchange an artifact reference without artifact content.
Exchange a message reference without message content.

RegisteredSource.description:
Server-accepted source registration facts.

A source may declare its capabilities and boundary in `SourceManifest`, but
only the accepting control plane assigns the effective trust profile and
organization scope represented here.

RunDescriptor.description: The immutable opening descriptor and current state for an Agent Run.

RunState.description: Lifecycle states for an Agent Run.

oneOf.description: Identity and source policy are being validated.
Terminal declarations and gaps are being reconciled.
The run is sealed without an unresolved required-source gap.
The run is sealed with at least one terminal or evidence gap.

RunStateTransition.description: An append-only lifecycle fact retained in an Agent Execution Record.

RuntimeAttribution.description: Explicit quality of a run-to-runtime correlation.

oneOf.description: An explicitly propagated identity was independently validated.
A non-unique heuristic selected one most likely runtime.
No qualifying runtime identity could be assigned.

RuntimeBinding.description: A versioned, evidence-backed relation between a run and runtime identity.

RuntimeBindingBasis.description: Evidence method used to establish a runtime correlation.

oneOf.description: A propagated runtime identifier was checked at an independent boundary.
A provider asserted the runtime relation inside its own boundary.
PID, time, path, argument, name, or comparable heuristic matching.

RuntimeBindingCandidate.description: One scored alternative retained for an ambiguous runtime relation.

RuntimeEffectBody.description: A host or managed-runtime effect represented by opaque identities.

RuntimeEffectKind.description: Runtime effect classes visible without retaining raw values.

oneOf.description: Container, Pod, VM, or infrastructure effect.

RuntimeIdentityKind.description: Runtime identity categories supported by the v0.1 binding contract.

SchemaVersion.description: Wire schema versions understood by this crate.

oneOf.description: All required semantic lifecycle evidence was observed.
Useful semantic evidence exists with a known required gap.
The semantic boundary is intentionally opaque.
Expected semantic evidence is not usable or available.

SourceCapability.description: A source capability declaration, not a per-run completeness claim.

oneOf.description: Agent, tool, or provider outcome claims.
Source health, loss, or terminal-position evidence.

SourceDiagnosticBody.description: A source-health observation without logs or captured content.

oneOf.description: The source recovered after a failure.

SourceEnvelope.description:
A source-supplied evidence contribution before server acceptance.

Organization scope, effective trust, ingest order, and ingest time are not
accepted from the source; they are present only in `AcceptedSourceEnvelope`.

oneOf.description: MCP, A2A, or other protocol evidence tap.

SourceManifest.description: Versioned declaration of an Evidence Source's identity and limits.

ToolInteractionBody.description: A tool interaction without raw arguments or results.

TrustProfile.description: The trust boundary under which source statements are interpreted.

oneOf.description: A source declared a fact without an independent observer.
A harness observed lifecycle or tool semantics.
A customer-controlled host boundary observed execution.
A vendor attests activity inside its own boundary.
The execution boundary is not observable by a qualifying source.
Expected trust evidence is missing, lost, or otherwise incomplete.

TypedEvidencePayload.description: Every structure-only v0.1 payload body accepted by the typed evidence seam.

oneOf.description: MCP or A2A protocol interaction evidence.
